metadata.description: Must match the clientName used in the main deployment.
Client (customer) Azure AD tenant ID. Used as the tenantId for locally-deployed playbooks.
Human-readable client name used in playbook emails and comments (e.g. 'Contoso Ltd').
SOC alert inbox. Switch to soc-ooh@conosco.com once the Halo workflow is live.
Primary security contact email at the client.
Secondary security contact email at the client.
Conosco managing-tenant subscription ID hosting the central playbooks.
Resource group in the Conosco tenant holding the central playbooks.
Conosco managing tenant ID. Used as the tenantId for cross-tenant playbook actions.

Deploy the four rules that call central playbooks in the Conosco tenant. Requires the Azure Security Insights app to hold Microsoft Sentinel Automation Contributor on the Conosco playbook RG (via Lighthouse) BEFORE deployment, or rule creation will fail. Set false to deploy only the local rules during testing.

resources.comments: Deploy Send_Incident_To_Conosco_SOC (workflow + connections) into the client RG. Authorise the Azureblob and Office365 connections in the portal after deployment.
resources.name: deploy-playbook-send-incident

metadata.description: Enter value for SendToEmail, should be soc-alerts@conosco.com. Once the Halo workflow is completed, change to soc-ooh@conosco.com

Compose_Email_response.inputs:

Alert providers

@{join(triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['additionalData']?['alertProductNames'], '

')}

@{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['description']}

Entities

resources.comments: Deploy NotifyContactsOnTruePositive (workflow + connections) into the client RG. Authorise the Teams and Office365 connections in the portal after deployment. NOTE: the true-positive classification check is performed inside this playbook, not in the automation rule.
resources.name: deploy-playbook-notify-true-positive

body.Subject: @{parameters('Client Name')}| Suspicious Activity Detected - @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['title']}
body.Body:
🔔 True Positive Security Incident

Please note that Conosco SOC has classified the following incident as a True Positive.

Incident ID: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['incidentNumber']}
Title: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['title']}
Entities Involved: @{variables('EntitiesList')}
Initial Findings: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['classificationComment']}
Time Detected: @{formatDateTime(convertTimeZone(triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['createdTimeUtc'], 'UTC', 'GMT Standard Time'), 'dd/MM/yyyy hh:mm tt') }
Severity: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['severity']}

We are conducting further investigation and will provide updates as soon as possible.

If you have any questions or require immediate assistance, please contact the SOC.

Best Regards,
Conosco SOC

resources.comments: Fires when an incident is closed. The NotifyContactsOnTruePositive playbook checks the classification internally and only notifies contacts when the closure is a true positive (classification is not a supported automation-rule condition property, so the gate lives in the playbook).
resources.name: [concat(variables('workspaceName'), '/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/', guid(variables('workspaceName'), 'rule-notify-true-positive'))]

properties.displayName: Notify Primary Contacts on True Positive
